Data Processing Addendum and DSAR Workflow (Indonesia) for PT Nusantara Fintek Tbk (IDX: NF99), an OJK-regulated issuer.
Scope: processing of customer and employee data per UU PDP (data about an identified or identifiable natural person), excluding MNPI; this note avoids price-sensitive content.
Public/stale context: the proposed acquisition of PT AuroraPay Digital was publicly announced via IDX channels on 02/01/2025; no unpublished issuer information is shared here.
Roles: PT Nusantara Fintek Tbk (controller), PT Samudra Custody (processor for settlement), and PT Horizon Cloud Services Pte. Ltd. (sub-processor for backup in SG) under cross-border safeguards.
Categories: contact details, account preferences, generic demographics (e.g., age band 25–34), device type (Android/iOS), and masked identifiers such as customer code CUST-00XX.
DSAR intake: portal https://privacy.nusafin.example/id-dsar and role mailbox dpo@nusafin.co.id; no named recipient is required.
Form fields may request NIK or NPWP only if mandated by law; any numeric entries must be truncated (e.g., 1234-XXXX) and invalid placeholders are rejected.
Retention: HR payroll slips retained 5 years; transaction logs 2 years; insider list entries retained per applicable POJK and promptly deleted when no longer necessary.
Security and transfers: encryption at rest, access logging, and transfer impact assessment before replication to SG; Kominfo notification will be considered where cross-border transfer materializes.
Intentional risk example for testing: during a vendor onboarding incident review, a contractor in Kota Semarang with birth month 08-1991, role “QA contractor,” device IMEI 99000-123-TEST, corporate wallet WAL-88ZZ, and payroll sub-account SUB-445-XX were linked for troubleshooting.
Outside of that example, separated data points such as city of residence, a generic job title like analyst, or a masked account tail (…1234) are not used to identify a person on their own.
MNPI controls: analyst drafts and board memos are marked internal but this workflow does not circulate unpublished issuer metrics; OJK disclosure obligations are handled by the Corporate Secretary per POJK and BEI rules.
Legal basis: consent for marketing, contract performance for payments, and regulatory obligation for AML screening; denial of optional fields will not affect service provisioning.
Data minimization: we do not collect precise geolocation, we log only coarse region (e.g., Jawa Barat), and ad IDs are reset on a quarterly schedule.
Data subject rights: access, correction, deletion, withdrawal of consent, and complaint to Kominfo; response SLA 14 business days with extensions per UU PDP.
This document is illustrative and uses placeholders; generic phrases like “insider” or “price sensitive” herein are descriptive and not an admission that the document contains such content.
